source_paragraph: PREVIOUS WORKS Among the relevant published studies in the log-based anomaly detection space, sequence-based is one of the most commonly used approaches. Therefore, there are limited relevant references as this study employs a point-based approach. Below are the relevant baselines from previous research: 1) Pylogsentiment is the first study which employs sentiment analysis-based anomaly detection on operat- ing system logs using GRU and GloVe embedding. 2) SentiLog , similar to , use a two-layered BiLSTM and GloVe embedding model to perform anomaly detection on parallel file system logs. 3) TransSentLog is a further development of and uses a two-layered transformer encoder which used two attention heads and GloVe embedding along with integrated gradients to add explainability to the trained model.

source_paragraph: I. INTRODUCTION I. INTRODUCTION The use of drones has become increasingly common in various applications, including aerial photography, surveying, and monitoring. However, drones can also be involved in incidents such as accidents, violations, or even crimes, making it necessary to investigate drone logs as part of forensic investigations. In a forensic investigation, drone logs are essential sources of information that can help investigators reconstruct the events leading up to an incident. Drone logs typically include information such as flight paths, altitude, speed, battery life, sensor data, and other telemetry information . One-stage detectors, such as YOLO and SSD, excel in speed and computational efficiency, making them ideal for real-time applications. For instance, YOLOv5 demonstrated remarkable performance in CRC detection with nearly perfect metrics—precision (99.071%), recall (98.064%), and mAP (98.8%)—allowing clinicians to analyze colonoscopy videos efficiently during live procedures . source_paragraph: DATASET The DEAP dataset has 32 participants with a composition of 50% male and 50% female samples . The stimulus used is a video with a duration of 60 seconds. The results of the EEG recording have a duration of 63 seconds, of which 3 seconds are the baseline. One participant has 40 recordings of the EEG trials. The results of the EEG trial recordings have been mea- sured using the Self-Assessment Manikin (SAM). SAM has four emotional dimensions, namely valence, arousal, domi- nance, and liking, with ratings 0-9. For valence and arousal classes, emotion labels are divided into two categories: low and high. CONCLUSION This research demonstrates that the YOLOv8 model, specifically the YOLOv8m variant, provides excellent results in detecting Peritoneal Carcinomatosis (PC) from the used laparoscopic image dataset. By utilizing and fine-tuning the pre-trained YOLOv8 weights, this model shows superior performance with a mean Average Precision (mAP50) of 0.871, precision of 0.893, recall of 0.785, and an F1 score of 0.84. The predicted images also show that the performance of YOLOv8 with pre-trained weights is able to predict images well. All YOLOv8 scales are accurate in making predictions, with no errors in image prediction. In contrast, without using pre-trained weights, YOLOv8n and YOLOv8s still make errors in prediction, with some lesions being missed.
